rationale: The cited paper is used directly in the results discussion to interpret a key finding: this study finds no significant effect of online display ads on offline purchase outcomes. The authors explicitly contrast that with prior evidence that banner ads can affect purchasing, citing Manchanda et al. (2006) as an example of effectiveness when advertising and purchase channels are aligned. This is more than a background list citation because it helps define a boundary condition for interpreting the focal paper's contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper qualifies prior banner-ad effectiveness evidence from Manchanda et al. (2006) by showing weaker effects in a different setting (online ads aimed at offline purchases). It argues that channel alignment is an important condition for when banner advertising translates into purchases.
